description: Catalog time-series and cross-sectional market anomalies, identify behavioral biases, and assess when apparent anomalies persist or fade under scrutiny

fields.name: bias_hypothesis
fields.label: loss_aversion | herding | overconfidence | information_cascade | anchoring | availability

time_series_anomalies.january_effect: Positive excess returns early January; smaller firms strongest
time_series_anomalies.overreaction: Past losers outperform past winners over 3-5 years
time_series_anomalies.weekend_effect: Monday returns historically negative vs Friday

cross_sectional_anomalies.value_effect: Low P/B or P/E firms outperform growth

other_anomalies.closed_end_discount: Funds trade at persistent discount to NAV
other_anomalies.earnings_surprise_drift: Post-earnings-announcement drift in direction of surprise
other_anomalies.ipo_long_run: IPOs under-perform seasoned peers for 3-5 years

behavioral_biases.herding: Following crowd into or out of positions
behavioral_biases.information_cascade: Ignoring private info to follow public actions
behavioral_biases.representativeness: Treating recent pattern as representative of future
behavioral_biases.mental_accounting: Treating money differently by source or label

anomaly_robustness.concerns: Data mining, survivorship, transaction costs often explain
anomaly_robustness.persistence: Some anomalies (momentum, value) persist out of sample
anomaly_robustness.implementation: After costs and capacity, alpha often shrinks or disappears

scan_anomaly.description: Scan returns data for named anomaly or behavioral pattern

errors.message: anomaly_category must be time_series, cross_sectional, or other

anti_patterns.rule: Do not chase an anomaly after it is widely published
anti_patterns.why: Arbitrage tends to reduce or eliminate anomalies once they become well known; hot reports are late signals

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore transaction costs and capacity
anti_patterns.why: Small-stock anomalies evaporate once scaled; modelled alpha is not investable alpha

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat behavioral biases as trading signals without risk management
anti_patterns.why: Individual bias-driven mispricings can persist arbitrarily long; position sizing and stops are required
